title: Oreogrammitis habbemensis
intro: Oreogrammitis habbemensis is a species of fern native to the island of New Guinea, classified within the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, pain, and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Oreogrammitis habbemensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Oreogrammitis habbemensis is often prepared as a decoction or a tea, and may also be used in the form of a topical cream or ointment to treat skin conditions.
taxonomy: Oreogrammitis habbemensis is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the division Pteridophyta. The classification of Oreogrammitis habbemensis places it within the class Polypodiopsida. This fern species is further divided into the subclass Polypodiidae and the order Polypodiales. As a member of the family Polypodiaceae, Oreogrammitis habbemensis has a more specific classification within the plant kingdom.
